description: Fix passthrough polygon transparency bug, add mood text to bubble, package GlobalInput as plugin, write project READMEs

userStories.title: Fix passthrough polygon merge to eliminate transparency holes
userStories.description: As a user, I want overlapping sprites (pet + furniture) to remain fully visible so the desktop doesn't show through them.
acceptanceCriteria: In _update_passthrough() in scripts/main.gd, refactor the polygon merge logic so overlapping rects produce a solid union with no interior holes
Use an iterative merge approach: start with the first polygon, then merge each subsequent polygon into the accumulated result using Geometry2D.merge_polygons()
When Geometry2D.merge_polygons() returns multiple polygons (disjoint islands), keep all of them in the accumulator and merge the next polygon against each island
Filter out rects with zero width or zero height before converting to polygons
Filter out duplicate or fully-contained rects that could create merge artifacts
The existing anchor-point path stitching (connecting disjoint islands through Vector2.ZERO for Linux stability) must still work correctly after the merge refactor
Verify: when pet sprite overlaps with sofa or any furniture, no transparent region appears — the overlapping area is fully opaque
Verify: clicking in the gap between two non-overlapping sprites still passes through to the desktop
No regression in passthrough for all existing UI elements: coin HUD, interaction menu, slide menu, shop, inventory, settings, pet selection, mood bubble, furniture, menu hint arrow
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The bug is confirmed via screenshot: where pet overlaps sofa, a transparent hole appears showing the desktop. The root cause is that Geometry2D.merge_polygons() can produce holes when polygons share interior regions and the winding order creates a subtraction. The fix must detect and discard inner/hole polygons (clockwise winding) from the merge result.

userStories.title: Add emotion text label to pet mood bubble
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a text label ('Sad', 'Neutral', 'Happy') in the pet's mood bubble so I can understand the pet's emotional state without guessing what the colors mean.
acceptanceCriteria: In scripts/pet.gd, add a Label node to the mood bubble layout (inside _bubble_panel) that displays the mood name
Text values: 'Sad' for PetMood.SAD, 'Neutral' for PetMood.NEUTRAL, 'Happy' for PetMood.HAPPY
Use an HBoxContainer inside _bubble_panel to arrange the mood icon (TextureRect) and the text label side by side
Text must be legible: use a font size of at least 12px, dark text color (e.g. Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2)) against the white bubble background
Text updates when mood changes (in _update_mood_display or equivalent)
The colored circle icon is still displayed alongside the text
Bubble panel size adjusts automatically to fit both icon and text (HBoxContainer handles this)
get_bubble_rect() still returns the correct rect for the new bubble size so passthrough remains correct
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The mood bubble is built programmatically in pet.gd around line 776-802. _bubble_panel is a PanelContainer, _bubble_icon is a TextureRect. The bubble shows/hides with fade animations via tweens.

userStories.title: Add exported texture properties for future emotion images
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to configure emotion images via exported node properties on the pet so images can be added later without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add three @export properties to pet.gd: sad_texture: Texture2D, neutral_texture: Texture2D, happy_texture: Texture2D — all defaulting to null
In the mood display update logic, when a texture is assigned (non-null) for the current mood, set _bubble_icon.texture to that exported texture instead of the generated colored circle
When the exported texture is null for a mood, fall back to the existing generated colored circle texture from _build_mood_textures()
The text label (from US-002) is always shown regardless of whether an image or colored circle is used

Add a code comment above the @export vars explaining: 'Assign custom mood images (e.g. expressive face sprites). When set, replaces the default colored circle for that mood. Leave null to use the default.'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is architecture-only — no actual emotion images are included. The textures are configured in the Godot editor by assigning resources to the exported properties on the PetSprite node.

userStories.title: Create GlobalInput plugin folder structure and plugin.cfg
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the GlobalInput GDExtension packaged as a standard Godot addons plugin with plugin.cfg so it can be enabled/disabled in Project Settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create addons/global_input/ directory

Create addons/global_input/plugin.cfg with: [plugin] name='GlobalInput', description='Captures system-wide keyboard and mouse input outside the Godot window', author='Amigos do Trabalho', version='1.0.0', script='plugin.gd'
Create addons/global_input/plugin.gd — an EditorPlugin script that adds GlobalInput as an autoload singleton in _enter_tree() and removes it in _exit_tree()
Move gdextension/src/ contents (global_input.h, global_input.cpp, global_input_linux.cpp, global_input_windows.cpp, register_types.h, register_types.cpp) to addons/global_input/src/
Move gdextension/SConstruct to addons/global_input/SConstruct
Move or copy compiled binaries from gdextension/bin/ to addons/global_input/bin/
Create addons/global_input/global_input.gdextension manifest with library paths relative to the plugin folder (e.g. res://addons/global_input/bin/...)
Remove the old root-level global_input.gdextension file and gdextension/ folder
Update project.godot to reference the new .gdextension path and enable the plugin
Verify GlobalInput singleton is still accessible from scripts/coin_system.gd (no code changes needed there if autoload name stays 'GlobalInput')
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The existing gdextension files are at /workspace/gdextension/ with manifest at /workspace/global_input.gdextension. The plugin.gd script should use add_autoload_singleton('GlobalInput', 'res://addons/global_input/global_input.gdextension') in _enter_tree() and remove_autoload_singleton('GlobalInput') in _exit_tree(). Check how the existing project references GlobalInput to ensure no breakage.

userStories.title: Write GlobalInput plugin README
userStories.description: As a developer adopting GlobalInput in another project, I want clear documentation on installation, building, and API usage.
acceptanceCriteria: Create addons/global_input/README.md
Section: Overview — what GlobalInput does (captures system-wide keyboard/mouse input outside Godot window for tracking user activity)
Section: Prerequisites — godot-cpp submodule, SCons, C++ compiler, platform deps (X11 + Xtst on Linux, user32 on Windows)
Section: Installation — copy addons/global_input/ to your project's addons/ folder, enable in Project > Project Settings > Plugins
Section: Building — SCons commands for Linux (scons platform=linux) and Windows (scons platform=windows), mention godot-cpp must be initialized
Section: API Reference — document all public methods: start_hooks(), stop_hooks(), get_key_count(), get_click_count(), reset_counts() with one-line descriptions
Section: Usage Example — GDScript snippet showing the polling pattern (timer, get counts, reset, convert to application logic)
Section: Platform Notes — Linux requires X11 (not Wayland), may need input group membership; Windows uses low-level hooks with message loop thread
Section: License — placeholder (e.g. 'MIT License — see LICENSE file')
Written in clear English, suitable for developers unfamiliar with the extension
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference the source files for accurate API docs: global_input.h for the public interface, global_input_linux.cpp for X11 details, global_input_windows.cpp for Windows hook details.

userStories.title: Write Amigos do Trabalho project README — overview, features, setup
userStories.description: As a new contributor, I want a project README covering the overview, feature list, and setup instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create README.md at the project root (/workspace/README.md)
Section: Project Overview — Amigos do Trabalho is a desktop pet companion that lives on your screen as a transparent overlay, with a brief description of the concept

Section: Features — bullet list covering: transparent desktop overlay, pixel-art pet with state machine AI, furniture system with shop/inventory, coin economy driven by keyboard/mouse activity, mood system, multi-monitor support, multi-language support, save/load persistence, pet selection system, furniture creator editor plugin
Section: Installation & Running — Godot 4.6+ requirement, clone repo, open in Godot, build GlobalInput native extension (link to plugin README), run the project

Section: Code Organization — directory structure table listing key directories (scripts/, scenes/, pet/, furniture/, addons/, assets/) and their purposes, plus a table of key script files (main.gd, pet.gd, coin_system.gd, furniture.gd, furniture_data.gd, etc.) with one-line descriptions
Written in clear English
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is part 1 of the README — covers overview and structure. Part 2 covers configuration and extensibility.

userStories.title: Write Amigos do Trabalho README — configuration, interactions, pet creation
userStories.description: As a new contributor, I want the README to explain how furniture is configured, how pet interactions work, and how to create new pets.
acceptanceCriteria: Append to the existing README.md created in US-006

Section: Furniture Configuration — explain FurnitureData resource properties (id, display_name, texture, coin_cost, walkable, jumpable, interaction_type, cooldown, display_scale, collision overrides, standing overrides, stackable), explain .tres files in furniture/data/, mention the Furniture Creator editor plugin for visual editing

Section: Pet Interactions & Animations — explain the pet state machine (IDLE, WALKING, FALLING, DRAGGED, INTERACTING, JUMP_PREP, JUMPING), how states transition, how interaction_type on furniture triggers INTERACTING state, how animations map to states (idle, walk, jump_prep, jump, fall, interact, dragged), the coin reward system on interaction, cooldown mechanics

Section: Creating New Pets — explain the SpriteFrames resource format, required animation names (idle, walk, jump_prep, jump, fall, interact, dragged), the directory convention (pet/data/petname.tres), auto-discovery by pet_selection_panel.gd, sprite sheet format expectations (rows/columns, frame size)
Section: Save Data — explain user://save_data.json format (coins, furniture_positions, inventory, selected_pet, language, monitor, etc.)
Section: GlobalInput Extension — brief overview with pointer to addons/global_input/README.md for details
Written in clear English, accessible to developers unfamiliar with the project
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This continues the README from US-006. Together they should form a comprehensive 3-5 page document. Reference actual file paths and property names from the codebase.

userStories.title: Delete bug screenshot folder
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the temporary bug-erase-me folder cleaned up after analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete the /workspace/bug-erase-me/ directory and its contents (overlaping_bug.jpg)
Verify the directory no longer exists
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Simple cleanup task. The bug screenshot has been analyzed and the fix is captured in US-001.
